Positive Verbatim Quote: I don't know how they cook their chicken but the skin is always crispy and the chicken is so tender and juicy. With every meal, they serve unlimited bean salad, coleslaw, cottage cheese, pickled beets, and corn fritters.
Negative Verbatim Quote: The chicken dinner must have been cooked in a different or even maybe old oil, the fat yummy corn fritters now resemble plain penny size dry donut holes, the veal liver size has been cut in half, the mashed potatoes are also dry and no longer rich and full of butter, even though we arrived early and had to be the first family at our table that day it was dirty, cottage cheese side is now a special request, the Farm in general seems run down, the cute craft stores are now filled with dollar store clutter and the wooden nickel chest for children is gone.
Notes: The restaurant permanently closed on December 30, 2018. The site was redeveloped into an apartment complex called NOVEL White Fence Farm, which opened in late 2023. The iconic barn was repurposed as the community clubhouse. While a Facebook page for 'White Fence Farm Restaurant' exists and posts about re-opening in 2026, it refers to the Romeoville, IL location, not the Lakewood, CO location.

Annie's Cafe and Bar at 3100 E Colfax Ave in Denver closed permanently on June 26, 2022 ([CBS Colorado](https://www.cbsnews.com/colorado/news/denver-annies-cafe-closing/), [Denver Westword](https://www.westword.com/food-drink/annies-cafe-closing-colfax-14262435/)). The building was sold and is now occupied by a location of Spice Room Neighborhood Indian Bistro ([Spice Room website](https://denverspiceroom.com)). Owner Peggy Anderson retired upon the closure ([5280 Magazine](https://5280.com/5-things-to-order-at-annies-cafe-before-it-says-goodbye/)). Status: CLOSED.

Positive Verbatim Quote: We came here to check out this iconic restaurant and it did not disappoint. It was crowded but we did not wait to be seated. The turnover is pretty quick as the servers are racing around. They are busy and quick but friendly. The vibe is great. Old school diner comfort food. Huge portions and delicious food.
Negative Verbatim Quote: Ewwwww.. The most unacceptable breakfast of all time..soggy toast .cold beacon . pancake served without syrup..tipid coffee..very expensive.. uneatable all way round ..fire the cook or just go under. .The place was empty with no other patrons, this was very telling...Do yourself a favor and eat elsewhere unless you enjoy bad cuisine.
Notes: Pete's Kitchen is part of Pete's Restaurants, which has 6 locations (Satire Lounge, Pete's Kitchen, Pete's University Park Cafe, Pete's Central One, Pete's Greek Town, and others listed in their story), making it a CHAIN. Google rating is 4.5, which is above the 3.7 threshold for DRIFT_FIT GOOD. Business is confirmed OPEN.
